
discussion_comments.comment_body: I reflected at https://github.com/TanStack/query/commit/1f5e12b209eab679973ea21eb60a6263d76c52a0
since we use real timers per default, please keep those times short (5ms and 10ms at most), or use fake timers and advance them how you want.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
this will make for a very long test. `fakeTimers` from vitest are probably the way to go here. We have some examples already, e.g. here:

https://github.com/TanStack/query/blob/1eebfc9d3355c411b16146cc8df76c18e63d59ad/packages/react-query/src/__tests__/useSuspenseQueries.test.tsx#L37

discussion_comments.comment_body: I improved the test code through a lot of thought to improve the test time, and the useFakeTimers you gave me were especially helpful. Thanks. 46057ca7076a40c7019346f7d0e4720d00f078f3

discussion_comments.comment_body: what does this do?
I wanted to test that hydrateSpy is not called over time.

I think this is better

```ts
await vi.runAllTimersAsync()
expect(hydrateSpy).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(0)
```

type only tests should please go towards the dedicated `.test-d.tsx` files:

- `packages/react-query/src/__tests__/useQueries.test-d.tsx`
- `packages/react-query/src/__tests__/useSuspenseQueries.test-d.tsx`
Okay, I will fix this.

commented_code:
+import { describe, expectTypeOf, it } from 'vitest'
+import { createQueries, queryOptions } from '..'
+import type { CreateQueryResult } from '..'
+
+describe('createQueries', () => {
+  it('should return correct data for dynamic queries with mixed result types', () => {
+    const Queries1 = {
+      get: () =>
+        queryOptions({
+          queryKey: ['key1'],
+          queryFn: () => Promise.resolve(1),
+        }),
+    }
+    const Queries2 = {
+      get: () =>
+        queryOptions({
+          queryKey: ['key2'],
+          queryFn: () => Promise.resolve(true),
+        }),
+    }
+
+    const queries1List = [1, 2, 3].map(() => ({ ...Queries1.get() }))
+    const result = createQueries(() => ({
+      queries: [...queries1List, { ...Queries2.get() }],
+    }))
+
+    expectTypeOf(result).toEqualTypeOf<
+      [
+        ...Array<CreateQueryResult<number, Error>>,
+        CreateQueryResult<boolean, Error>,
+      ]
+    >()
+
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition
+    if (!result[0]) {
+      throw new Error('Type check failure')
+    }

+import { describe, expectTypeOf, it } from 'vitest'
+import { createQueries, queryOptions } from '..'
+import type { CreateQueryResult } from '..'
+
+describe('createQueries', () => {
+  it('should return correct data for dynamic queries with mixed result types', () => {
+    const Queries1 = {
+      get: () =>
+        queryOptions({
+          queryKey: ['key1'],
+          queryFn: () => Promise.resolve(1),
+        }),
+    }
+    const Queries2 = {
+      get: () =>
+        queryOptions({
+          queryKey: ['key2'],
+          queryFn: () => Promise.resolve(true),
+        }),
+    }
+
+    const queries1List = [1, 2, 3].map(() => ({ ...Queries1.get() }))
+    const result = createQueries(() => ({
+      queries: [...queries1List, { ...Queries2.get() }],
+    }))
+
+    expectTypeOf(result).toEqualTypeOf<
+      [
+        ...Array<CreateQueryResult<number, Error>>,
+        CreateQueryResult<boolean, Error>,
+      ]
+    >()
+
+    // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-condition
+    if (!result[0]) {
+      throw new Error('Type check failure')
+    }
discussion_comments.comment_body:
that’s not how type-tests work. They don’t have a runtime - the `it` function never gets executed by vitest.

it should be fine to just remove that.
Agreed, I will remove it! thx

discussion_comments.comment_body: Is this testing what it says? It might also be the case that `usePrefetchQuery` didn't fetch, but `useSuspenseQuery` did. Not sure if it necessarily needs fixing, but thought I should point it out.

discussion_comments.comment_body: Added two error scenarios in the test file

This is why use toEqualTypeOf (Error type)
with toEqualTypeOf, We can strictly type check
Remove unnecessary expectTypeNotAny by expectTypeOf().toEqualTypeOf
